mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts signals aggregate demand weakness outpacing supply adjustments, leading to earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% month-over-month while HOUST falls > 5.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rapid increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor inflation expectations and instead trigger liquidity crunches, causing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation tightening periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expansion signals robust corporate revenue growth, which directly supports higher equity valuations and positive forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window despite positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates below zero percent month-over-month, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints reverse, triggering a positive mean-reversion trade in equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change falls below zero
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant positive skew when industrial production contracts.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with rising unemployment indicates a labor market paradox where job creation is offset by workforce participation drops, signaling structural economic weakness that depresses equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint payroll-unemployment signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, surging real estate activity amplifies leverage cycles, making equity markets highly sensitive to subsequent demand shocks and producing negative forward returns over a week.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-credit housing periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10).
